placemaking_private_property: community gathering space on private property

residential_table_131_04B.1: Development of a mobilehome park in any RS or RX zone is subject to Section 143.0302.
residential_table_131_04B.2: Development of a mobilehome park in the RM zones is subject to Section 143.0302.
residential_table_131_04B.3: The 40,000 square feet includes all indoor and outdoor areas that are devoted to the recreational use; it does not include customer parking areas.
residential_table_131_04B.4: Two guest rooms are permitted for visitor accommodations per the specified square footage of lot area required per dwelling unit (maximum permitted density), as indicated on Table 131-04G.
residential_table_131_04B.5: See Section 131.0423 (Commercial Use Regulations for Residential Zones).
residential_table_131_04B.6: A guest quarters or habitable accessory building is permitted in accordance with Section 141.0307 only as an accessory use to a single dwelling unit.
residential_table_131_04B.7: Mobile food trucks are permitted by right on the property of a school, university, hospital, religious facility, previously conforming commercial premises in a residential zone, or construction site. Mobile food trucks on any other premises are subject to the limited use regulations set forth in Section 141.0612.
residential_table_131_04B.8: Development of a small lot subdivision is permitted in accordance with Section 143.0365.
residential_table_131_04B.9: Development of multiple dwelling units permitted in accordance with Sections 141.0305 and 141.0319.
residential_table_131_04B.10: Visitor accommodation lodging for uses other than a SRO hotel or SRO hotel room shall not be used for a stay of more than 30 consecutive days.
residential_table_131_04B.11: Student housing subject to limited use regulations.

commercial_table_131_05B.1: Uses shall not begin operating before 6:00 a.m. or continue operating later than 12:00 midnight in Commercial-Neighborhood (CN) zones.
commercial_table_131_05B.3: Only office furniture, appliances, and equipment establishments are permitted. The gross floor area occupied by these uses shall not exceed 2,500 square feet for each premises.
commercial_table_131_05B.4: Live entertainment after 12:00 midnight is not permitted in the Commercial-Neighborhood (CN) zones, unless approval of a deviation is granted with a Planned Development Permit in accordance with Section 126.0602(b)(1). All uses or activities shall be conducted entirely within an enclosed building and front onto the primary street with no uses or commercial activities conducted outdoors in the rear yard adjacent to residentially-zoned properties.
commercial_table_131_05B.5: The sale of alcoholic beverages is not permitted as a primary use.
commercial_table_131_05B.6: The gross floor area occupied by these uses shall not exceed 2,500 square feet for each premises.
commercial_table_131_05B.8: These activities shall be located solely within an enclosed building that does not exceed 7,500 square feet of gross floor area. Activities that would require a permit from the Hazardous Materials Management Division of the County of San Diego or from the San Diego Air Pollution Control District are not permitted.
commercial_table_131_05B.9: The 40,000 square feet includes all indoor and outdoor areas that are devoted to the recreational use; it does not include customer parking areas.
commercial_table_131_05B.10: This use is not allowed within the Coastal Overlay Zone, except that assembly and entertainment uses may be incorporated as an accessory use to visitor accommodations.
commercial_table_131_05B.11: Development of a large retail establishment is subject to Section 143.0302.
commercial_table_131_05B.12: Within the Coastal Overlay Zone, instructional studios are not permitted on the ground floor in the CV-1-1 or CV-1-2 zone.
commercial_table_131_05B.13: Permitted in CV zones where the gross floor area occupied by an individual retail sales establishment would not exceed 2,500 square feet.
commercial_table_131_05B.14: Specialized practice massage establishments are permitted only as an accessory use in the CV-1-1 and CV-1-2 zones.
commercial_table_131_05B.15: This use is permitted as a limited use subject to a mobile food truck permit in accordance with Section 123.0603 and the limited use regulations in Section 141.0612.
commercial_table_131_05B.16: Eating and drinking establishments abutting residential development located in a residential zone may operate only between 6:00 a.m. and 12:00 midnight. All uses or activities shall be conducted entirely within an enclosed building and front onto the primary street with no uses or commercial activities conducted outdoors in the rear yard adjacent to residentially-zoned properties.
commercial_table_131_05B.17: Tasting rooms are only permitted as an accessory use to a beverage manufacturing plant.
commercial_table_131_05B.18: This use is not allowed within the Coastal Overlay Zone.
commercial_table_131_05B.20: Within the beach impact area of the Parking Impact Overlay Zone, off-street parking spaces shall be provided at a ratio of no less than one off-street parking space for every 250 square feet of gross floor area.
commercial_table_131_05B.21: Visitor accommodation lodging for uses other than a SRO hotel or SRO hotel room shall not be used for a stay of more than 30 consecutive days.

industrial_table_131_06B.1: A regional and corporate headquarters establishment shall have a gross floor area of at least 40,000 square feet.
industrial_table_131_06B.2: Household and office furniture, appliances, and equipment sales establishments shall occupy an area of at least 5,000 square feet in gross floor area unless the sales are of items that are manufactured on the same premises.
industrial_table_131_06B.11: The 40,000 square feet includes all indoor and outdoor areas that are devoted to the recreational use; it does not include customer parking areas.
industrial_table_131_06B.12: Only one regional and corporate headquarters establishment is permitted on an individual parcel of land.
industrial_table_131_06B.13: Development of a large retail establishment is subject to Section 143.0302.
industrial_table_131_06B.16: Eating and drinking establishments abutting residential development located in a residential zone may operate only between 6:00 a.m. and 12:00 midnight.
industrial_table_131_06B.17: Distribution facilities are permitted in the IP-1-1 zone only within the Otay Mesa Community Plan area.
industrial_table_131_06B.18: Tasting rooms are only permitted as an accessory use to a beverage manufacturing plant.
industrial_table_131_06B.21: Funeral and Mortuary Services are not permitted within floodplains located in the Coastal Overlay Zone.
industrial_table_131_06B.22: A maximum of 10 percent of the gross floor area on the premises may be used for retail sales.
industrial_table_131_06B.23: Moving and Storage Facilities are prohibited where the applicable land use plan identifies the premises as Prime Industrial Lands. This is not applicable to premises located in the Marine Corps Air Station Miramar Airport Land Use Compatibility Plan Accident Potential Zone 1.
